text:
in all those 2m I didn't see a single log update, I had no idea what the thing was doing -- I want it to tell me what its going on more proactively [...] Assistant: Now add the capture lifecycle events to the feed so it narrates each phase:
 [...] 1. **Live phase in the headline** — the "current" box now narrates the actual stage (`starting` → `extracting claims` → `tagging authority` → `routing to guides` → `reconciling guides` → `writing wiki` → `rebuilding index`), driven by the session's own events, with a `· waiting on model` marker between an `llm.request` and its response. This is the piece that stays informative *through* a silent LLM call.
2. **Feed narrates the lifecycle** — added `capture.start / extract / authority_tagging / route / agent_done / done` feed lines, so each phase prints a line (e.g. `⛏ extract 12 raw claim(s)`, `🧭 route → 3 guide(s)`) instead of a blank feed until the first wiki write. [...] it shows me new guide lines a bunch of times, but it doesn't show me any of the claims that were discovered [...] also, selecting is weird, instead of showing the row selected it stops-rendering the rows under the current row -- its very strange [...] Assistant: Now I can see complaint #3's root cause clearly. The windowing conflates "scroll offset" with "cursor":

```rust
let end = total.saturating_sub(view.feed_scroll);   // shrinks the window's BOTTOM as you scroll up
let start = end.saturating_sub(feed_inner_h);
let cursor_idx = total.saturating_sub(view.feed_scroll.max(1)).min(total.saturating_sub(1));
```

When you press Up (feed_scroll=1), `end` drops to `total-1`, so the newest row stops rendering — and the cursor_idx (`total-1`) is actually *outside* the rendered window. Scrolling further keeps peeling rows off the bottom. That's exactly "it stops rendering the rows under the current row." Let me read the key/event loop to fix the model coherently. [...] - **#3** (render): bottom-anchored window, cursor moves within it; unify `cursor_idx` into one helper used by render + Enter + clamp.
- **#2** (archeologist): per-session transcript map (ungated, insert-if-absent on every `llm.response` sidecar); Enter on a "Reading conversation" line loads & renders the EXTRACT transcript; add scroll to the detail overlay. [...] **1. "Shows New guide a bunch of times, but no claims"** — `src/capture.rs`
The reconcile emitted `wiki.create` based on the op *label* (`op.op == "create"`), not on whether a guide was actually created. When the LLM labels every statement of one brand-new guide as "create" (your "Relay-Enforced Event Chains" ×10), the first genuinely creates it and the rest fall through to the add-statement path — but all still fired `wiki.create`. Now I detect *genuine* creation with a flag set inside the locked closure (`existing.is_none()`), and skip the event entirely on error. The 10 dup "New guide" lines collapse to **1 New guide + 9 claim lines** — fixing both halves at once (the math checks out: 1 create + 9 adds + 1 REPL op = the "11 op(s)" you saw). [...] **3. Selection "stops rendering rows under the current row"** — `src/archeologist.rs`
The window's bottom edge was tied to the scroll offset (`end = total - feed_scroll`), so every Up peeled a row off the bottom and the cursor was actually *outside* the rendered window. Rewrote it as a bottom-anchored window where the cursor moves *within* the window and only scrolls it once the cursor climbs above the top. Unified the cursor-index math into one `feed_cursor_idx` helper used by render, Enter, and scroll so they can't drift. [...] User: when I scroll past the viewport I don't see the position since the container doesn't scroll down [...] First, extract `feed_window` and use it in render so the invariant is testable:
 [...] Assistant: Now use `feed_window` in render and add the position indicator to the feed title: [...] Assistant: Now the live-drift fix — hold scrolled/paused position as new lines arrive:
 [...] **1. Position drift while scrolled up** (`src/archeologist.rs:1457`) — the old code only held your scroll position when *paused* (`if view.feed_paused && !was_at_bottom`). So if you scrolled up during a live run without pausing, every incoming event grew the feed but left `feed_scroll` unchanged — the view slid out from under you and the selection drifted to a different line. Now: `if view.feed_paused || !was_at_bottom`, bumping `feed_scroll` in lock-step with the growing feed so the cursor stays on the same logical line. Live-follow resumes when you scroll back to the bottom. (Verified the ring-cap case too: when the buffer drops an old row, the `+1` still keeps the cursor on the right item.) [...] **2. No position readout** ("I don't see the position") — the feed title now shows `feed · line 47/210` whenever you're scrolled up, so you can see exactly where the cursor is. This helps **whether or not** the run is live. [...] **3. Refactor** — extracted the window math into a testable `feed_window()` helper used by render, locked by the invariant test above.
